title: The Egyptian Museum (Tahrir)
description: The oldest archaeological museum in the Middle East, housing the treasures of Tutankhamun.

title: National Museum of Egyptian Civilization (NMEC)
description: The first museum exclusively dedicated to Egyptian civilization across ages (home of Royal Mummies).

title: Mosque of Ibn Tulun
description: The oldest mosque in the city surviving in its original form, known for its spiral minaret.

title: Gayer-Anderson Museum
description: An art museum housed in two historic Ottoman houses joined by a bridge.

title: Sayyidah Zeinab Mosque
description: A historic and significant mosque believed to contain the shrine of Zeinab, granddaughter of the Prophet.

title: Citadel of Saladin
description: A medieval Islamic fortification on Mokattam hill.

title: Mosque of Muhammad Ali
description: Also known as the Alabaster Mosque, situated in the Citadel.

title: Sultan Hassan Mosque and Madrasa
description: A massive mosque and madrasa located near the Citadel.

title: Al-Rifa'i Mosque
description: Located opposite Sultan Hassan Mosque, burial place of the royal family.

title: Bab Zuweila
description: One of the three remaining gates in the walls of the Old City of Cairo.

title: Mosque of Al-Muayyad Shaykh
description: Located next to Bab Zuweila, built by Sultan Al-Muayyad.

title: Bayt Al-Suhaymi
description: A historic Ottoman-era house museum.

title: Al-Azhar Mosque
description: One of the oldest mosques and universities constructed in Cairo.

title: Mosque of Al-Hakim bi-Amr Allah
description: A major Islamic religious site named after the Fatimid Caliph.

title: Bab al-Futuh
description: Gate of Conquests, one of three remaining gates in the city wall.

title: Bab al-Nasr
description: Gate of Victory, a massive fortified gate.

title: Complex of Qalawun
description: Includes a mosque, a madrasa, and a mausoleum, known for beautiful architecture.

title: Madrasa of Al-Nasir Muhammad
description: Built by Sultan Al-Nasir Muhammad adjacent to Qalawun complex.

title: Madrasa of Sultan Barquq
description: Religious complex comprising a mosque-madrasa.

title: Sabil-Kuttab of Katkhuda
description: An excellent example of Ottoman architecture, combining a fountain and school.

title: Al-Aqmar Mosque
description: Known for its unique facade design.

title: Beshtak Palace
description: A historic palace and museum.

title: Hammam Inal
description: A historic public bathhouse.

title: The Hanging Church
description: Saint Virgin Mary's Coptic Orthodox Church, built on top of the Babylon Fortress gatehouse.

title: Church of Saints Sergius and Bacchus
description: Thought to be built on the spot where the Holy Family rested.

title: Ben Ezra Synagogue
description: Ideally located in Old Cairo, one of the oldest synagogues.

title: Mosque of Amr ibn al-Aas
description: The first mosque built in Egypt and Africa.

title: Nilometer (Rhoda Island)
description: Structure used to measure the Nile River's water level.

title: Manial Palace
description: A distinctively decorated palace built by Prince Mohammed Ali Tewfik.

title: Abdeen Palace
description: A historic Cairo palace and one of the official residences of the President.

title: Baron Empain Palace
description: A distinct and historic Hindu temple-inspired mansion.

title: Obelisk of Senusret I
description: The oldest standing obelisk in its original position.

title: Tree of the Virgin Mary
description: A sycamore tree where the Holy Family is said to have rested.

title: Tell al-Hisn
description: Archaeological mound marking the site of ancient Heliopolis.

title: Amir Taz Palace
description: A palace reflecting the Mamluk architectural style.

title: Blue Mosque (Aqsunqur Mosque)
description: Famous for its blue iznik tiles.

title: Khayrbak Complex
description: A funerary complex including a mosque and mausoleum.

title: Umm al-Sultan Sha'ban Madrasa
description: A madrasa and mausoleum complex.

title: Bayt Al-Razzaz
description: A palace complex created from two houses.

title: Sabil of Muhammad Ali Pasha
description: A historic sabil (water dispensary) on Al-Muizz street.

title: Wikala of Al-Ghouri
description: A caravansarai and market complex.

title: Mashhad of Sayyida Ruqayya
description: A mausoleum for the daughter of Ali ibn Abi Talib.

title: Dome of Shajar al-Durr
description: Mausoleum of the famous female Sultan.

title: Helwan Japanese Garden
description: A garden designed with Asian influences.

title: Rokn Farouk
description: A royal rest house located on the Nile.

title: Church of Saint George
description: A Greek Orthodox church within the Babylon Fortress.

title: Sabil-Kuttab of Tussun Pasha
description: A public water fountain and Quranic school.

title: Beit El-Sinnari
description: A historic house used by the Institut d'Égypte.

title: Mausoleum of Imam al-Shafi'i
description: Shrine of the founder of the Shafi'i madhhab.

title: Khan el-Khalili
description: A famous souq and bazaar district.

title: Fortress of Babylon
description: Ancient Roman fortress around which Coptic Cairo grew.

title: Sakakini Palace
description: A Rococo style palace built by Habib Sakakini Pasha.
